prompt: BUILD a new Dark Factory skill: `comprehend-visualise` (Phase 1 of the Comprehend->Visualise subsystem). FIRST read backlog/2026-06-07-comprehend-visualise-PLAN.md and backlog/2026-06-07-comprehend-visualise-process.md for full context. GOAL of the skill: given {target (a repo/folder path, local or remote-over-SSH), intent, output_location}, produce TWO artifacts and STOP -- (1) a grounded comprehension DIGEST of the target system, and (2) a render-BRIEF (JSON) that a downstream Mochaccino render step will consume. DO NOT render anything (rendering = Phase 2, Mochaccino's job). CREATE `.claude/skills/comprehend-visualise/SKILL.md` (+ references/ only if needed). Follow David's voice: trigger-only description ('Use when...'), terse, stack-agnostic, no marketing prose. The SKILL.md must document this procedure: (1) ENUMERATE the target: packages, databases/schemas, key components, entry points; read `git log` for the last 14 days. If target is remote, read read-only over SSH. (2) FAN OUT parallel readers (sub-agents), one per package/subsystem, each returning a structured CITED mini-digest (what it is, key files, deps, data shapes), tagging verified(cite)/inference/unknown. (3) MERGE into ONE comprehension digest (grounded, file-cited). (4) SHAPE SELECTION -- map the system to Mochaccino/Shelly shapes with a one-line justification each: architecture->layer-stack, packages->card-grid, DB/schema->matrix, git-history->timeline, a key flow->lifecycle-flow. Reference the real Shelly catalog at appydave-plugins/appydave/skills/shelly. (5) EMIT the render-BRIEF as JSON: { target:{path, commit_sha}, output_location, visualisations:[ {shape, title, source_files[], shows} ] } -- path+commit_sha are the provenance Mochaccino's Peter will stamp as meta.source. Write artifacts to <output_location>/digest.md and <output_location>/brief.json (default output_location = <target>/.comprehend/). CONSTRAINTS: read-only on the target; no rendering; stack-agnostic; provenance = path + commit_sha. SELF-TEST (must do): run the skill once against a SMALL LOCAL target to prove the comprehend->digest->brief path works end-to-end. Use target = experiments/watchtower-board (small, local, real). Cap at ~4 visualisations. Write the self-test digest.md + brief.json under experiments/watchtower-engine/proof/cv-selftest/. Confirm brief.json is valid JSON with >=3 visualisations each having shape+title+source_files+shows. PROOF: write experiments/watchtower-engine/proof/comprehend-visualise-phase1.md (<=8 lines): what the skill does, where it lives, the self-test target + result (digest line count, # visualisations in brief), and the ONE thing Phase 2 (Mochaccino render) needs from the brief. Then complete run-next-workflow bookkeeping (run record + move to done/ + reports/ handback). Report ONE line.
